description: Create a content slide on how operational resilience is addressed within organizational risk appetite, using a two-column layout with explanatory text on the left and a horizontal bar chart on the right.

header.kicker: SECOND ANNUAL EY/EIU GLOBAL INSURANCE RISK MANAGEMENT SURVEY
header.headline: How is operational resilience addressed within the organizational risk appetite?

items.text: For 67% of respondents, operational resilience is addressed within organizational risk appetite indirectly (either through qualitative statements/analysis and supporting commentary within risk appetite reporting or via inclusion of related non-financial quantitative board risk appetite metrics).
Only 5% of CROs say operational resilience is not addressed in the risk appetite.
CROs and other industry executives show growing interest in principle-based approaches to operational resilience.
Firms are developing frameworks with formal definitions and rankings of critical services based on customer impacts, mapping processes to services, and defining specific impact tolerances.

right.title: How is operational resilience addressed within the organizational risk appetite?

series.label: Indirectly, through qualitative analysis and supporting commentary within risk appetite reporting
Indirectly, via inclusion of related non-financial quantitative board risk appetite metrics (e.g., third party, information security, technology) and cascaded management metrics
Directly, with inclusion of specific resilience-focused quantitative board risk appetite metrics and cascaded management metrics

Directly, with explicit acknowledgment and/or mention in the qualitative risk appetite statement and inclusion of specific resilience-focused quantitative board risk appetite metrics and cascaded management metrics
